Build succeeds with no errors. All 13 changes are implemented:

1. **`polygon.js`** — Added `signedPolygonArea` helper
2. **`defaults.js`** — Added `ENDPOINT_MERGE_TOLERANCE` (5mm) and `MIN_ROOM_AREA` (0.1 m²)
3. **`roomDetection.js`** — Created planar graph cycle detection (merge endpoints → build adjacency → sort by angle → trace faces → filter by signed area)
4. **`models.js`** — Updated `createRoom` to accept `points[]`, compute `area` and `centroid`
5. **`ProjectProvider.jsx`** — Added `ROOMS_SET` bulk-replace action
6. **`RoomRenderer.jsx`** — Renders filled polygons with name + area labels, highlights selected
7. **`SvgCanvas.jsx`** — Passes `selectedId` to RoomRenderer
8. **`SelectionOverlay.jsx`** — Dashed polygon outline for selected rooms
9. **`selectHandler.js`** — Room hit testing (pointInPolygon) + Delete key support
10. **`Toolbar.jsx`** — "Detect Rooms" button that runs detection and bulk-replaces rooms
11. **`Sidebar.jsx`** — Rooms listing section with click-to-select
12. **`PropertiesPanel.jsx`** — RoomProperties component (name, area, color) + delete support
13. **`deserialize.js`** — Backfills `points`/`area` for old saves

message.content: what's the use of snap btw?

content.text: Snap constrains cursor positions to the grid and to existing wall endpoints when drawing/placing elements. It makes it easy to align walls precisely to grid increments and connect walls at exact shared endpoints — which is also important for room detection, since it relies on walls meeting at the same coordinates.

This plan adds automatic room detection from wall topology, polygon rendering with area labels, and full selection/editing integration. 6→ 7→## Files to Change 8→ 9→| File | Action | What | Risk | 10→|------|--------|------|------| 11→| `src/geometry/roomDetection.js` | **CREATE** | Planar graph cycle detection algorithm | High | 12→| `src/geometry/polygon.js` | MODIFY | Add `signedPolygonArea` helper | Very low | 13→| `src/domain/models.js` | MODIFY | Add `points`/`area` to `createRoom` | Low | 14→| `src/domain/defaults.js` | MODIFY | Add `ENDPOINT_MERGE_TOLERANCE` constant | Very low | 15→| `src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx` | MODIFY | Add `ROOMS_SET` bulk-replace action | Low | 16→| `src/renderers/RoomRenderer.jsx` | MODIFY | Render filled polygons + name + area labels | Medium | 17→| `src/renderers/SelectionOverlay.jsx` | MODIFY | Add room selection dashed outline | Low | 18→| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` | MODIFY | Pass `selectedId` to RoomRenderer | Very low | 19→| `src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js` | MODIFY | Add room hit testing (pointInPolygon) + delete | Low | 20→| `src/ui/Toolbar.jsx` | MODIFY | Add "Detect Rooms" button | Low | 21→| `src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` | MODIFY | Add Rooms listing section | Low | 22→| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` | MODIFY | Add RoomProperties component | Low | 23→| `src/persistence/deserialize.js` | MODIFY | Backfill `points`/`area` for old saves | Very low | 24→ 25→--- 26→ 27→## Change 1: Add `signedPolygonArea` to `polygon.js` 28→ 29→The existing `polygonArea` returns absolute value. Room detection needs signed area to distinguish interior faces (CW in screen coords) from the outer boundary (CCW). 30→ 31→```js 32→export function signedPolygonArea(points) { 33→ let area = 0; 34→ const n = points.length; 35→ for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) { 36→ const j = (i + 1) % n; 37→ area += points[i].x * points[j].y; 38→ area -= points[j].x * points[i].y; 39→ } 40→ return area / 2; 41→} 42→``` 43→ 44→--- 45→ 46→## Change 2: Create `src/geometry/roomDetection.js` 47→ 48→Pure geometry module — no React. Single export: `detectRooms(walls, mergeTolerance)`. 49→ 50→### Algorithm 51→ 52→**Step A — Build planar graph:** 53→1. Collect all wall endpoints, merge nearby ones within `mergeTolerance` (default 5mm). Simple O(n²) scan — apartment plans have few walls. 54→2. Build adjacency list: each wall adds two directed half-edges `(start→end)` and `(end→start)`. Skip degenerate/duplicate edges. 55→ 56→**Step B — Sort edges by angle:** 57→For each vertex, sort its adjacency by `Math.atan2(neighbor.y - vertex.y, neighbor.x - vertex.x)` ascending. 58→ 59→**Step C — Build next-half-edge map:** 60→For directed half-edge `(u→v)`: at vertex `v`, find index of `u` in `v`'s sorted adjacency. The next half-edge is `v → adjacency[v][(idx - 1 + degree) % degree]`. This traces the face to the left of the half-edge. 61→ 62→**Step D — Trace cycles:** 63→For each unvisited half-edge, follow next-pointers until returning to start. Collect vertex indices → polygon. 64→ 65→**Step E — Filter:** 66→- Discard cycles with < 3 vertices 67→- Discard cycles with area < `MIN_ROOM_AREA` (100,000 mm² = 0.1 m²) 68→- Compute signed area: keep only cycles where `signedPolygonArea > 0` (CW in screen Y-down coords = interior faces). The outer boundary has negative signed area and is automatically excluded. 69→ 70→**Returns:** `Array<{ points: [{x,y}], area: number }>` — each detected room polygon with absolute area in mm². 71→ 72→### Known Limitations (acceptable for basic support) 73→- T-junctions (wall ending mid-wall without shared endpoint) are not detected as connections — users must draw walls that meet at endpoints (the snap system already encourages this) 74→- No wall intersection splitting 75→ 76→--- 77→ 78→## Change 3: Update `createRoom` in `models.js` 79→ 80→```js 81→export function createRoom(name = 'Room', points = [], color = ROOM_COLOR) { 82→ const area = polygonArea(points); 83→ const centroid = points.length >= 3 ? polygonCentroid(points) : { x: 0, y: 0 }; 84→ return { 85→ id: generateId('room'), 86→ name, 87→ points: points.map(p => ({ x: p.x, y: p.y })), 88→ labelPosition: { ...centroid }, 89→ color, 90→ area, 91→ }; 92→} 93→``` 94→ 95→Import `polygonArea` and `polygonCentroid` from `@/geometry/polygon`. The old `labelPosition`-only signature is replaced — no existing callers to break. 96→ 97→--- 98→ 99→## Change 4: Add `ENDPOINT_MERGE_TOLERANCE` to `defaults.js` 100→ 101→```js 102→export const ENDPOINT_MERGE_TOLERANCE = 5; // mm 103→export const MIN_ROOM_AREA = 100000; // mm² (0.1 m²) 104→``` 105→ 106→--- 107→ 108→## Change 5: Add `ROOMS_SET` action to `ProjectProvider.jsx` 109→ 110→Add after the `ROOM_DELETE` case: 111→ 112→```js 113→case 'ROOMS_SET': 114→ return updateFloor(state, action.floorId, f => ({ 115→ ...f, 116→ rooms: action.rooms, 117→ })); 118→``` 119→ 120→This bulk-replaces all rooms on a floor — used by the detect button. 121→ 122→--- 123→ 124→## Change 6: Rewrite `RoomRenderer.jsx` 125→ 126→Render each room as: 127→1. Filled `` with `room.color`, `fillOpacity={0.4}`, `pointerEvents: 'all'` for click targeting 128→2. Room name `` at `labelPosition`, offset slightly above center 129→3. Area `` below name, formatted as `(area / 1_000_000).toFixed(1) + ' m²'` 130→ 131→Text has `pointerEvents: 'none'` so clicks pass through to polygon. Accept `selectedId` prop to highlight selected room with slightly higher opacity. 132→ 133→Only render polygon if `room.points?.length >= 3`; legacy rooms (no points) render name-only as before. 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→## Change 7: Room selection overlay in `SelectionOverlay.jsx` 138→ 139→Add after the `window` case: 140→ 141→```jsx 142→if (selectedType === 'room') { 143→ const room = floor.rooms.find(r => r.id === selectedId); 144→ if (!room?.points?.length) return null; 145→ const points = room.points.map(p => `${p.x},${p.y}`).join(' '); 146→ return ( 147→ 155→ ); 156→} 157→``` 158→ 159→--- 160→ 161→## Change 8: Pass `selectedId` to RoomRenderer in `SvgCanvas.jsx` 162→ 163→Change line 198: 164→```jsx 165→ 166→``` 167→ 168→--- 169→ 170→## Change 9: Room hit testing in `selectHandler.js` 171→ 172→Add room hit test **after** walls (lower priority) in the `hitTest` function, before `return null`: 173→ 174→```js 175→for (const room of floor.rooms) { 176→ if (room.points?.length >= 3 && pointInPolygon(modelPos, room.points)) { 177→ return { id: room.id, type: 'room' }; 178→ } 179→} 180→``` 181→ 182→`pointInPolygon` is already imported. ## Summary: Door and Window Placement Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the modular apartment planner codebase, **doors and windows are fully implemented and well-integrated throughout the entire application**. Here's the detailed breakdown:

### 1. Placement Handlers (Both Implemented and Complete)

**File: `/src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js`**
- Implements full door placement workflow with wall detection (500mm radius)
- Projects cursor position onto nearest wall and clamps offset within wall bounds
- Creates door on mouse click via `DOOR_ADD` dispatch
- Provides cursor feedback: 'copy' when valid, 'not-allowed' when invalid
- Escape key cancels tool and reverts to select mode

**File: `/src/editor/handlers/windowPlaceHandler.js`**
- Mirror implementation of door handler for windows
- Identical wall detection and offset clamping logic
- Creates window on mouse click via `WINDOW_ADD` dispatch
- Same cursor and keyboard behavior as doors

### 2. Renderers (Both Implemented with Full SVG Graphics)

**File: `/src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`**
- Renders doors as SVG with detailed architectural representation:
  - White masking rectangle to cut through walls
  - Door leaf line (thickness based on door.width)
  - Swing arc (dashed line) showing open direction
  - Respects door.openDirection ('left' or 'right')
  - Uses `doorOutlineOnWall()` geometry function for positioning

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis report.

## Comprehensive Analysis: Door and Window Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the modular apartment planner codebase, here's a detailed technical report on the current door and window implementation:

### 1. **Current Door Object Structure** (`src/domain/models.js`)

```javascript
{
  id: 'door_m1abc_1',           // Unique ID (prefix_timestamp36_counter36)
  wallId: 'wall_xyz',            // Reference to parent wall
  offset: 1500,                  // Position along wall (mm from wall start)
  width: 900,                    // Door width in mm (default)
  openDirection: 'left' | 'right' // Swing direction
}
```

### 2. **Current Window Object Structure** (`src/domain/models.js`)

```javascript
{
  id: 'win_m1abc_1',             // Unique ID
  wallId: 'wall_xyz',             // Reference to parent wall
  offset: 2000,                  // Position along wall (mm)
  width: 1200                    // Window width in mm (default)
  // NO variant property - only one window type supported
}
```

### 3. **SVG Rendering Details**

#### **Door SVG Elements** (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`)

The door is rendered as a group (`<g>`) containing:

1. **White rectangle (polygon)** - Masks the wall behind the door opening
   - 4 corner points (p1, p2, p3, p4) from `doorOutlineOnWall()`
   - Fill: white, stroke: none

2. **Door swing visualization** - Transforms at wall angle:
   - **Door leaf line**: A simple `<line>` from start point to end point
     - X1: 0, Y1: 0
     - X2: 0, Y2: flipSign * -door.width (negative swing direction)
     - Stroke: wall color, strokeWidth: 2px, vectorEffect non-scaling
   
   - **Swing arc**: A `<path>` with SVG arc command
     - Command: `M ${door.width} 0 A ${door.width} ${door.width} 0 0 ${sweepFlag} 0 ${flipSign * -door.width}`
     - sweepFlag: 1 if openDirection='left', 0 if 'right'
     - This creates a quarter-circle arc showing the door swing path
     - Stroke: dashed (4px dash, 3px gap), 1px strokeWidth

**Key rendering transformation:**
```javascript
flipSign = door.openDirection === 'right' ? -1 : 1
// Applied to Y coordinates to flip door direction
```

#### **Window SVG Elements** (`src/renderers/WindowRenderer.jsx`)

Windows use a much simpler representation:

1. **White rectangle (polygon)** - Masks wall
   - Same 4 corner points (p1, p2, p3, p4)

2. **Three parallel lines across window gap**:
   - Lines at offsets: -0.3, 0, 0.3 (as fractions of wall thickness)
   - Middle line (offset 0): strokeWidth 2px (thicker)
   - Outer lines: strokeWidth 1px
   - All calculated using wallDirection and perpendicular vectors
   - Creates a cross-hatch visual pattern

### 4. **Geometry Calculations** (`src/geometry/wallGeometry.js`)

Both `doorOutlineOnWall()` and `windowOutlineOnWall()` return:

```javascript
{
  center,           // Point at center of opening along wall
  start,            // Left point (center - halfWidth direction)
  end,              // Right point (center + halfWidth direction)
  p1,               // Top-left corner (start + perp offset)
  p2,               // Top-right corner (end + perp offset)
  p3,               // Bottom-right corner (end - perp offset)
  p4,               // Bottom-left corner (start - perp offset)
  angle,            // Wall angle in radians
  width             // Opening width in mm
  openDirection     // (door only) 'left' | 'right'
}
```

**Key coordinate calculations:**
- All values in millimeters
- Wall direction: normalized vector from wall.start → wall.end
- Wall perpendicular: 90° rotation of direction
- Opening outline: extends full wall thickness (halfThick offset perpendicular to wall)
- Position on wall: `center = wall.start + direction * offset`

### 5. **How `openDirection` Is Used**

- **Door creation**: Default `'left'`
- **Rendering**:
  - Affects flipSign calculation
  - Controls arc sweep flag in SVG
  - Determines which way the swing arc curves
- **UI Toggle**: PropertiesPanel has "Toggle Swing Direction" button that flips left ↔ right
- **Stored directly** in door object, persisted to localStorage

### 6. **Placement & Selection Logic**

**Door Placement** (`src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js`):
- Finds nearest wall within 500mm radius
- Projects mouse position onto wall
- Clamps offset to valid range: [halfWidth, wallLength - halfWidth]
- Creates door with default width (900mm) and openDirection ('left')
- No preview of door type variant

**Window Placement** (`src/editor/handlers/windowPlaceHandler.js`):
- Same logic as door placement
- Default width 1200mm
- No type property to preview

**Selection & Dragging** (`src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js`):
- Hit test uses polygon collision on door/window outline (p1-p4)
- Doors and windows tested before walls (higher priority)
- Drag allows repositioning along wall (clamps to valid range)
- Can be deleted with Delete/Backspace

### 7. **State Management**

**ProjectProvider** (`src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx`):
- DOOR_ADD: appends door object to floor.doors
- DOOR_UPDATE: spreads updates into door object
- DOOR_DELETE: removes by ID
- Same pattern for WINDOW_ADD, WINDOW_UPDATE, WINDOW_DELETE
- Wall deletion cascades: removes all doors/windows on that wall

### 8. **Persistence**

**Serialization** (`src/persistence/serialize.js`):
- Direct JSON serialization of entire project (no schema transformation)

**Deserialization** (`src/persistence/deserialize.js`):
- Basic version validation (version must be 1)
- Backfill logic for old saves (only handles rooms, not doors/windows)
- No migration logic for adding new door/window fields

---

## **Changes Required for Door Variants** (Double, Sliding, Pocket)

### Model Layer (`src/domain/models.js`):
- Add `doorType: 'swing' | 'double' | 'sliding' | 'pocket'` property
- Update `createDoor()` signature to accept doorType
- Update defaults.js with new constants

### Rendering (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`):
- **Swing**: Current code (single arc)
- **Double**: Two parallel leaf lines + two arcs (one per direction)
- **Sliding**: Two overlapping rectangles with arrows showing direction
- **Pocket**: Single leaf line + arc, but recessed into wall thickness visualization

### Geometry (`src/geometry/wallGeometry.js`):
- `doorOutlineOnWall()` returns outline same for all types
- Rendering logic handles type-specific visualization

### UI (`src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx`):
- Add dropdown for doorType selection in DoorProperties
- Show appropriate toggle/controls per type (e.g., sliding door might show direction instead of swing)

### Persistence:
- Add migration in `deserialize.js` to backfill `doorType: 'swing'` for old doors
- Update version if schema changes significantly

---

## **Changes Required for Window Variants** (Casement, Fixed, Sliding)

### Model Layer (`src/domain/models.js`):
- Add `windowType: 'fixed' | 'casement' | 'sliding'` property
- Update `createWindow()` signature

### Rendering (`src/renderers/WindowRenderer.jsx`):
- **Fixed**: Current three-line pattern (unchanged)
- **Casement**: Add small leaf lines at edges (like door swing visualization)
- **Sliding**: Show overlapping panes with direction indicator

### UI (`src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx`):
- Add dropdown for windowType in WindowProperties
- Optional: add rotation/direction property for casement/sliding

### Persistence:
- Add migration for `windowType: 'fixed'` backfill

---

## **File Locations Summary**

| Purpose | File |
|---------|------|
| Door/Window models | `/src/domain/models.js` |
| Default constants | `/src/domain/defaults.js` |
| SVG rendering | `/src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`, `/WindowRenderer.jsx` |
| Geometry helpers | `/src/geometry/wallGeometry.js` |
| Wall outline/direction | `/src/geometry/point.js`, `/line.js` |
| UI properties panel | `/src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` |
| Placement handlers | `/src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js`, `/windowPlaceHandler.js` |
| Selection logic | `/src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js` |
| State reduction | `/src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx` |
| Persistence | `/src/persistence/serialize.js`, `/deserialize.js` |

All coordinates use **millimeters** throughout the system. The SVG canvas applies a 0.1x default zoom and uses `vectorEffect="non-scaling-stroke"` to keep strokes consistent across zoom levels.

## Comprehensive Analysis: Door and Window Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the modular apartment planner codebase, here's a detailed technical report on the current door and window implementation:

### 1. **Current Door Object Structure** (`src/domain/models.js`)

```javascript
{
  id: 'door_m1abc_1',           // Unique ID (prefix_timestamp36_counter36)
  wallId: 'wall_xyz',            // Reference to parent wall
  offset: 1500,                  // Position along wall (mm from wall start)
  width: 900,                    // Door width in mm (default)
  openDirection: 'left' | 'right' // Swing direction
}
```

### 2. **Current Window Object Structure** (`src/domain/models.js`)

```javascript
{
  id: 'win_m1abc_1',             // Unique ID
  wallId: 'wall_xyz',             // Reference to parent wall
  offset: 2000,                  // Position along wall (mm)
  width: 1200                    // Window width in mm (default)
  // NO variant property - only one window type supported
}
```

### 3. **SVG Rendering Details**

#### **Door SVG Elements** (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`)

The door is rendered as a group (`<g>`) containing:

1. **White rectangle (polygon)** - Masks the wall behind the door opening
   - 4 corner points (p1, p2, p3, p4) from `doorOutlineOnWall()`
   - Fill: white, stroke: none

2. **Door swing visualization** - Transforms at wall angle:
   - **Door leaf line**: A simple `<line>` from start point to end point
     - X1: 0, Y1: 0
     - X2: 0, Y2: flipSign * -door.width (negative swing direction)
     - Stroke: wall color, strokeWidth: 2px, vectorEffect non-scaling
   
   - **Swing arc**: A `<path>` with SVG arc command
     - Command: `M ${door.width} 0 A ${door.width} ${door.width} 0 0 ${sweepFlag} 0 ${flipSign * -door.width}`
     - sweepFlag: 1 if openDirection='left', 0 if 'right'
     - This creates a quarter-circle arc showing the door swing path
     - Stroke: dashed (4px dash, 3px gap), 1px strokeWidth

**Key rendering transformation:**
```javascript
flipSign = door.openDirection === 'right' ? -1 : 1
// Applied to Y coordinates to flip door direction
```

#### **Window SVG Elements** (`src/renderers/WindowRenderer.jsx`)

Windows use a much simpler representation:

1. **White rectangle (polygon)** - Masks wall
   - Same 4 corner points (p1, p2, p3, p4)

2. **Three parallel lines across window gap**:
   - Lines at offsets: -0.3, 0, 0.3 (as fractions of wall thickness)
   - Middle line (offset 0): strokeWidth 2px (thicker)
   - Outer lines: strokeWidth 1px
   - All calculated using wallDirection and perpendicular vectors
   - Creates a cross-hatch visual pattern

### 4. **Geometry Calculations** (`src/geometry/wallGeometry.js`)

Both `doorOutlineOnWall()` and `windowOutlineOnWall()` return:

```javascript
{
  center,           // Point at center of opening along wall
  start,            // Left point (center - halfWidth direction)
  end,              // Right point (center + halfWidth direction)
  p1,               // Top-left corner (start + perp offset)
  p2,               // Top-right corner (end + perp offset)
  p3,               // Bottom-right corner (end - perp offset)
  p4,               // Bottom-left corner (start - perp offset)
  angle,            // Wall angle in radians
  width             // Opening width in mm
  openDirection     // (door only) 'left' | 'right'
}
```

**Key coordinate calculations:**
- All values in millimeters
- Wall direction: normalized vector from wall.start → wall.end
- Wall perpendicular: 90° rotation of direction
- Opening outline: extends full wall thickness (halfThick offset perpendicular to wall)
- Position on wall: `center = wall.start + direction * offset`

### 5. **How `openDirection` Is Used**

- **Door creation**: Default `'left'`
- **Rendering**:
  - Affects flipSign calculation
  - Controls arc sweep flag in SVG
  - Determines which way the swing arc curves
- **UI Toggle**: PropertiesPanel has "Toggle Swing Direction" button that flips left ↔ right
- **Stored directly** in door object, persisted to localStorage

### 6. **Placement & Selection Logic**

**Door Placement** (`src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js`):
- Finds nearest wall within 500mm radius
- Projects mouse position onto wall
- Clamps offset to valid range: [halfWidth, wallLength - halfWidth]
- Creates door with default width (900mm) and openDirection ('left')
- No preview of door type variant

**Window Placement** (`src/editor/handlers/windowPlaceHandler.js`):
- Same logic as door placement
- Default width 1200mm
- No type property to preview

**Selection & Dragging** (`src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js`):
- Hit test uses polygon collision on door/window outline (p1-p4)
- Doors and windows tested before walls (higher priority)
- Drag allows repositioning along wall (clamps to valid range)
- Can be deleted with Delete/Backspace

### 7. **State Management**

**ProjectProvider** (`src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx`):
- DOOR_ADD: appends door object to floor.doors
- DOOR_UPDATE: spreads updates into door object
- DOOR_DELETE: removes by ID
- Same pattern for WINDOW_ADD, WINDOW_UPDATE, WINDOW_DELETE
- Wall deletion cascades: removes all doors/windows on that wall

### 8. **Persistence**

**Serialization** (`src/persistence/serialize.js`):
- Direct JSON serialization of entire project (no schema transformation)

**Deserialization** (`src/persistence/deserialize.js`):
- Basic version validation (version must be 1)
- Backfill logic for old saves (only handles rooms, not doors/windows)
- No migration logic for adding new door/window fields

---

## **Changes Required for Door Variants** (Double, Sliding, Pocket)

### Model Layer (`src/domain/models.js`):
- Add `doorType: 'swing' | 'double' | 'sliding' | 'pocket'` property
- Update `createDoor()` signature to accept doorType
- Update defaults.js with new constants

### Rendering (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`):
- **Swing**: Current code (single arc)
- **Double**: Two parallel leaf lines + two arcs (one per direction)
- **Sliding**: Two overlapping rectangles with arrows showing direction
- **Pocket**: Single leaf line + arc, but recessed into wall thickness visualization

### Geometry (`src/geometry/wallGeometry.js`):
- `doorOutlineOnWall()` returns outline same for all types
- Rendering logic handles type-specific visualization

### UI (`src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx`):
- Add dropdown for doorType selection in DoorProperties
- Show appropriate toggle/controls per type (e.g., sliding door might show direction instead of swing)

### Persistence:
- Add migration in `deserialize.js` to backfill `doorType: 'swing'` for old doors
- Update version if schema changes significantly

- Add `windowType: 'fixed' | 'casement' | 'sliding'` property

|---------|------|

| SVG rendering | `/src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`, `/WindowRenderer.jsx` |

Let me present the detailed implementation plan. --- ## Implementation Plan: Door Variants and Window Variants ### Overview This plan adds three door types (`swing`, `double`, `sliding`) and three window types (`standard`, `casement`, `fixed`) to the modular apartment planner. The design follows the existing patterns throughout: factory functions in models, reducer-based state, SVG rendering, and property editing in the sidebar panel. The user changes the type after placing (not before), which avoids adding new toolbar tools and keeps the workflow identical to today. --- ### Design Decisions (Answering the 6 Questions) **1. What properties to add to each model?** Add a single `type` string field to each: - **Door**: `type` with values `'swing'` (default), `'double'`, `'sliding'`. No additional properties needed because: - `double` reuses `width` (total opening width; each leaf is `width / 2`) and `openDirection` (ignored, always opens symmetrically outward). - `sliding` reuses `width` and `openDirection` (determines slide direction for the arrow indicator). - **Window**: `type` with values `'standard'` (default), `'casement'`, `'fixed'`. Additional property: - `casement` reuses `width` and needs no extra field; it will show a small swing arc similar to a door leaf, opening outward on one side. It can reuse the concept of `openDirection` -- so add `openDirection` (default `'left'`) to the window model as well, for casement windows. For `standard` and `fixed` types, `openDirection` is ignored. **2. How to render each variant?** All variants share the same wall-masking white polygon (steps p1-p4 are identical). Only the decorative SVG inside the `` changes. **Door variants (all use `doorOutlineOnWall` for positioning):** - **`swing`** (current): Unchanged. Single leaf line + dashed arc from `info.start`. - **`double`**: Two leaf lines from center, each `width/2` long, opening in opposite directions. Two quarter-arc paths from center, mirrored. Rendered in a `` at `info.center` with rotation `angleDeg`. - Left leaf: line from (0,0) to (0, -width/2); arc from (width/2, 0) to (0, -width/2) - Right leaf: line from (0,0) to (0, width/2); arc from (width/2, 0) to (0, width/2) - **`sliding`**: No arc. A thick line representing the door panel (slightly shorter than width), plus a small arrow indicating slide direction. Rendered as a `` offset slightly from center in the slide direction, plus a small triangle/arrow ``. **Window variants (all use `windowOutlineOnWall` for positioning):** - **`standard`** (current): Unchanged. Three parallel lines at fractions -0.3, 0, +0.3. - **`casement`**: A single line across the gap (at center, fraction 0) plus a small triangular arc on one side (like a door swing but smaller, using wall thickness as the "opening" radius). The arc side determined by `openDirection`. - **`fixed`**: Two diagonal lines forming an X across the window opening (corner-to-corner of p1-p2-p3-p4), plus the center line. This is the architectural convention for fixed glazing. **3. What UI changes in PropertiesPanel?** - **DoorProperties**: Add a "Type" selector (cycle button or `` element styled consistently with `InputField`. This is simpler than a cycle button and clearly shows all options. **4. How to handle placement?** Place with defaults (door: `swing`, window: `standard`), then change type in the properties panel after selecting. This is the simplest approach: - No new toolbar buttons needed. - No changes to `doorPlaceHandler` or `windowPlaceHandler` needed (they already create with defaults). - The user can quickly place multiple items and adjust types as needed. - Consistent with how `openDirection` already works (placed as `left`, changed in properties panel). **5. Backward compatibility in deserialize.js?** Add a migration step in `deserializeProject` that backfills missing fields: - For each door missing `type`, set `type = 'swing'`. - For each window missing `type`, set `type = 'standard'`. - For each window missing `openDirection`, set `openDirection = 'left'`. This is identical to the existing room backfill pattern. No version bump needed since `serializeProject` just wraps the data object -- the new fields naturally serialize, and old saves without them get backfilled on load. **6. What about defaults for variant-specific properties?** - `DOOR_WIDTH = 900` stays as the default for all door types. Double doors at 900mm total means 450mm per leaf, which is architecturally reasonable (common double doors are 1200-1800mm total, but 900mm is a fine default the user can adjust). - Add `DOUBLE_DOOR_WIDTH = 1400` as a suggested constant but do NOT use it as auto-default; keep placement simple with a single width. The user adjusts width in the properties panel. - `WINDOW_WIDTH = 1200` stays as the default for all window types. - `SLIDING_DOOR_WIDTH = 900` not needed separately; same reasoning. Actually, keeping it simpler: no new default constants at all. All types use the same `DOOR_WIDTH` and `WINDOW_WIDTH` defaults. Users adjust width after placement if needed. --- ### Step-by-Step Implementation #### Step 1: Update Domain Models (`src/domain/models.js`) Add `type` to both factory functions: ```js export function createDoor(wallId, offset, width = DOOR_WIDTH, openDirection = 'left', type = 'swing') { return { id: generateId('door'), wallId, offset, width, openDirection, type }; } export function createWindow(wallId, offset, width = WINDOW_WIDTH, type = 'standard', openDirection = 'left') { return { id: generateId('win'), wallId, offset, width, type, openDirection }; } ``` No changes to `defaults.js` needed. The valid type strings could be documented as constants if desired, but plain strings are simpler and match the existing `openDirection` pattern (which is just `'left'`/`'right'` strings). #### Step 2: Update Deserialization (`src/persistence/deserialize.js`) Add backfill logic after the existing room backfill block (around line 29): ```js // Backfill missing door/window type fields for old saves for (const floor of project.floors) { if (!floor.doors) floor.doors = []; for (const door of floor.doors) { if (!door.type) door.type = 'swing'; } if (!floor.windows) floor.windows = []; for (const win of floor.windows) { if (!win.type) win.type = 'standard'; if (!win.openDirection) win.openDirection = 'left'; } } ``` No changes to `serialize.js` -- it already serializes the full project object, so new fields are included automatically. #### Step 3: Update Door Renderer (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`) Refactor the rendering inside `doors.map(...)` to branch on `door.type`. Extract each variant into a helper function for clarity. The wall-masking polygon stays the same for all types. Only the content of the inner `` changes. **`renderSwingDoor(door, info)`** -- current logic, unchanged: - Leaf line from (0,0) to (0, flipSign * -door.width) - Arc path **`renderDoubleDoor(door, info)`** -- new: - `` translated to `info.center` and rotated by `angleDeg` - Left leaf: line (0,0) to (0, -halfWidth) - Right leaf: line (0,0) to (0, halfWidth) - Left arc: `M ${halfWidth} 0 A ${halfWidth} ${halfWidth} 0 0 1 0 ${-halfWidth}` - Right arc: `M ${halfWidth} 0 A ${halfWidth} ${halfWidth} 0 0 0 0 ${halfWidth}` Where `halfWidth = door.width / 2`. The transform origin is `info.center` (the midpoint of the door opening), not `info.start`. **`renderSlidingDoor(door, info)`** -- new: - `` translated to `info.start` and rotated by `angleDeg` - Panel line: a slightly thick line (strokeWidth 3) from (0, 0) to (door.width, 0) offset slightly perpendicular to suggest the panel is offset from the wall plane - Arrow indicator: small arrow `` pointing in the slide direction (based on `openDirection`) - For left: arrow pointing negative along wall direction - For right: arrow pointing positive - Alternative simpler approach: just render the panel as a line with a small perpendicular tick at the leading edge. Design an implementation plan for adding door variants and window variants to the modular apartment planner.

## Current State

Doors and windows are fully implemented with a single type each. Here's the relevant architecture:

### Door Model (`src/domain/models.js`)
```js
export function createDoor(wallId, offset, width = DOOR_WIDTH, openDirection = 'left') {
  return { id: generateId('door'), wallId, offset, width, openDirection };
}
```

### Window Model (`src/domain/models.js`)
```js
export function createWindow(wallId, offset, width = WINDOW_WIDTH) {
  return { id: generateId('win'), wallId, offset, width };
}
```

### Door Rendering (`src/renderers/DoorRenderer.jsx`)
Each door renders:
1. White polygon masking the wall (p1-p4 from doorOutlineOnWall)
2. A `<g>` with transform at opening center/angle containing:
   - A leaf `<line>` from (0,0) to (0, flipSign * -door.width)
   - A swing arc `<path>` with SVG arc command: `M ${door.width} 0 A ${door.width} ${door.width} 0 0 ${sweepFlag} 0 ${flipSign * -door.width}`
   - flipSign = openDirection === 'right' ? -1 : 1
   - sweepFlag = openDirection === 'left' ? 1 : 0

### Window Rendering (`src/renderers/WindowRenderer.jsx`)
Each window renders:
1. White polygon masking the wall
2. Three parallel lines across window gap at offsets -0.3, 0, +0.3 of wall thickness. Middle line has strokeWidth 2, outer lines strokeWidth 1.

### Geometry (`src/geometry/wallGeometry.js`)
`doorOutlineOnWall(wall, door)` and `windowOutlineOnWall(wall, win)` return: `{ center, start, end, p1, p2, p3, p4, angle, width, openDirection? }`

### Properties Panel (`src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx`)
- DoorProperties: width, offset, openDirection toggle, delete
- WindowProperties: width, offset, delete

### Defaults (`src/domain/defaults.js`)
```
DOOR_WIDTH = 900
WINDOW_WIDTH = 1200
```

### Placement handlers
doorPlaceHandler and windowPlaceHandler find nearest wall, project offset, create with defaults.

### Deserialization (`src/persistence/deserialize.js`)
Validates floors/walls exist, backfills room fields. No door/window-specific migration.

## Desired Variants

**Doors:**
- `swing` (current single-leaf door — the default)
- `double` (two leaves swinging outward from center)
- `sliding` (slides along wall, no swing)

**Windows:**
- `standard` (current three-line pattern — the default)
- `casement` (hinged, shows open arc like a mini door)
- `fixed` (cannot open, simple X or solid lines pattern)

## Constraints
- Keep it simple, no over-engineering
- All coordinates in mm, SVG uses vectorEffect non-scaling-stroke
- Must be backward-compatible with existing saves (old doors get type='swing', old windows get type='standard')
- Follow existing patterns (factory functions, reducer actions, CSS modules)
- The door/window renderers already use a `doors.map(door => ...)` pattern with per-item SVG groups

## Questions to answer
1. What properties to add to each model?
2. How to render each variant (specific SVG elements)?
3. What UI changes needed in PropertiesPanel?
4. How to handle placement (should the user pick type before placing, or change type after placing)?
5. Backward compat in deserialize.js?
6. What about defaults for variant-specific properties (e.g., double door default width)?

lines: -Rooms partially exist in the codebase (model factory, reducer actions, text-only renderer) but lack polygon boundaries, area computation, auto-detection from walls, selection, and properties editing. This plan adds automatic room detection from wall topology, polygon rendering with area labels, and full selection/editing integration.

+Doors and windows are fully implemented with a single type each: swing doors and standard (three-line) windows. This plan adds variant types — double and sliding doors, casement and fixed windows — so the user can place a default type and then change it via the properties panel.
-| `src/geometry/roomDetection.js` | **CREATE** | Planar graph cycle detection algorithm | High |
-| `src/renderers/RoomRenderer.jsx` | MODIFY | Render filled polygons + name + area labels | Medium |
-| `src/renderers/SelectionOverlay.jsx` | MODIFY | Add room selection dashed outline | Low |
-| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` | MODIFY | Pass `selectedId` to RoomRenderer | Very low |
-| `src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js` | MODIFY | Add room hit testing (pointInPolygon) + delete | Low |
-| `src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` | MODIFY | Add Rooms listing section | Low |
-| `src/persistence/deserialize.js` | MODIFY | Backfill `points`/`area` for old saves | Very low |
+- **Type selection happens after placement** — the user places a door/window with defaults, then changes type in the properties panel. No new toolbar buttons or placement handlers needed.
+- **One new field per model**: `door.type` (`'swing'`|`'double'`|`'sliding'`) and `win.type` (`'standard'`|`'casement'`|`'fixed'`). Windows also gain `openDirection` (for casement).
+- **Rendering branches inside existing renderers** — extract variant-specific SVG into helper components, keep the wall-masking polygon shared.
+- **No new constants or geometry helpers** — all variants reuse the same `doorOutlineOnWall`/`windowOutlineOnWall` positioning.
+| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` | MODIFY | Add type `<select>` dropdowns, conditional direction toggle |
-The existing `polygonArea` returns absolute value. Room detection needs signed area to distinguish interior faces (CW in screen coords) from the outer boundary (CCW).
+No changes needed to: placement handlers, ProjectProvider (spread-update handles new fields), selectHandler, Toolbar, SvgCanvas, SelectionOverlay, wallGeometry, serialize, defaults.
-Pure geometry module — no React. Single export: `detectRooms(walls, mergeTolerance)`.
-1. Collect all wall endpoints, merge nearby ones within `mergeTolerance` (default 5mm). Simple O(n²) scan — apartment plans have few walls.
-2. Build adjacency list: each wall adds two directed half-edges `(start→end)` and `(end→start)`. Skip degenerate/duplicate edges.
-For each vertex, sort its adjacency by `Math.atan2(neighbor.y - vertex.y, neighbor.x - vertex.x)` ascending.

-For directed half-edge `(u→v)`: at vertex `v`, find index of `u` in `v`'s sorted adjacency. The next half-edge is `v → adjacency[v][(idx - 1 + degree) % degree]`. This traces the face to the left of the half-edge.
-For each unvisited half-edge, follow next-pointers until returning to start. Collect vertex indices → polygon.
-- Discard cycles with area < `MIN_ROOM_AREA` (100,000 mm² = 0.1 m²)
-- Compute signed area: keep only cycles where `signedPolygonArea > 0` (CW in screen Y-down coords = interior faces). The outer boundary has negative signed area and is automatically excluded.
-**Returns:** `Array<{ points: [{x,y}], area: number }>` — each detected room polygon with absolute area in mm².
-- T-junctions (wall ending mid-wall without shared endpoint) are not detected as connections — users must draw walls that meet at endpoints (the snap system already encourages this)
-  const centroid = points.length >= 3 ? polygonCentroid(points) : { x: 0, y: 0 };
-    points: points.map(p => ({ x: p.x, y: p.y })),

+  return { id: generateId('door'), wallId, offset, width, openDirection, type };
-Import `polygonArea` and `polygonCentroid` from `@/geometry/polygon`. The old `labelPosition`-only signature is replaced — no existing callers to break.
+export function createWindow(wallId, offset, width = WINDOW_WIDTH, type = 'standard', openDirection = 'left') {
+  return { id: generateId('win'), wallId, offset, width, type, openDirection };
+Add after the existing room backfill block:
-This bulk-replaces all rooms on a floor — used by the detect button.
-2. Room name `<text>` at `labelPosition`, offset slightly above center

-Text has `pointerEvents: 'none'` so clicks pass through to polygon. Accept `selectedId` prop to highlight selected room with slightly higher opacity.
-Only render polygon if `room.points?.length >= 3`; legacy rooms (no points) render name-only as before.
+- Two leaves from center, each `width/2` long, opening in opposite perpendicular directions
+- Left leaf: `(0,0)` → `(0, -halfWidth)`, arc sweeping from `(halfWidth, 0)` to `(0, -halfWidth)`
+- Right leaf: `(0,0)` → `(0, halfWidth)`, arc sweeping from `(halfWidth, 0)` to `(0, halfWidth)`
+- Direction arrow: thin line at `y = flipSign * -12` from `0.2*width` → `0.8*width`, with a small V-shaped arrowhead at the trailing end
+Refactor similarly. Masking polygon stays the same.

+- Uses a `<g transform="translate/rotate">` like doors (at `info.start`, rotated by angle)
+- Hinge leaf: short line from `(0,0)` → `(0, flipSign * -win.width * 0.3)`
+- Small dashed arc: radius `win.width * 0.3`, quarter-circle from pane to leaf end
-Add room hit test **after** walls (lower priority) in the `hitTest` function, before `return null`:
+- Two diagonal lines forming an X: `info.p1→info.p3` and `info.p2→info.p4`, strokeWidth 1
